back_translation: For the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. A song.
May God arise; may His enemies be scattered; may those who hate Him flee from before His face.
Drive them away like smoke blown by the wind; may the wicked perish before God like wax melting before the fire.
But the righteous will be glad and rejoice before God; they will be joyful with gladness.
Sing to God; sing praises to His name; exalt Him who rides above the clouds—His name is the LORD—rejoice before His face.
God, who dwells in His holy habitation, is a father of the fatherless and a defender of widows.
God settles the solitary in families; He leads those who are imprisoned out to prosperity; but the rebellious must dwell in a parched land.
O God, when You went out before Your people, when You marched through the wilderness, Selah
the earth shook, and the heavens poured down rain before God, the God of Mount Sinai, before God, the God of Israel.
O God, You sent down abundant rain; You refreshed Your weary inheritance.
Your flock dwelt in that land; O God, by Your goodness You provided for the poor.
The Lord gives the command, and a great company of women proclaim the news:
'Kings and their armies flee in haste, and the woman who keeps the house divides the plunder.'
Though you lie down among the sheepfolds, the wings of the dove are covered with silver, and its feathers with glittering gold.
When the Almighty scattered the kings in the land, it was like snow falling on Mount Zalmon.
The mountain of God is Mount Bashan; a mountain of many peaks is Mount Bashan.
O mountain of many peaks, why do you gaze with envy? This is the mountain that God chose for His dwelling, where the LORD will dwell forever.
The chariots of God are tens of thousands; the Lord comes among them from Mount Sinai into the sanctuary.
You ascended on high; You led away many captives; You received gifts from men, even from the rebellious, that the LORD God might dwell there.
Blessed be the Lord, who bears our burden day by day, the God of our salvation. Selah
Our God is a God of deliverance, and escape from death belongs to the LORD, our Lord.
Surely God will shatter the heads of His enemies, the hairy crown of the one who walks in his guilt.
The Lord said, 'I will bring them back from Bashan; I will bring them up from the depths of the sea,'
that you may dip your foot in the blood of the enemy, and that the tongues of your dogs may have their share of the enemy too.
O God, they have seen Your procession—the procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
The singers go before, the musicians follow after, among the young women playing tambourines.
Bless God in the great assembly; bless the LORD, you who descend from the fountain of Israel.
There is Benjamin, the youngest, leading them; the princes of Judah in their throng; the princes of Zebulun and Naphtali.
O God, command Your strength; O God, show the power that You have worked for us.
Because of Your temple at Jerusalem, kings will bring gifts to You.
Rebuke the beast in the reeds, the herd of bulls among the calves of the nations, until it submits and brings bars of silver. Scatter the nations who delight in war.
Envoys will come from Egypt; Cush will hurry to stretch out her hands to God.
O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; sing praises to the Lord. Selah
To Him who rides above the highest heavens of old; behold, He utters His mighty voice.
Proclaim that power belongs to God; His majesty is over Israel, and His might is in the skies.
O God, You are awesome in Your sanctuary; the God of Israel Himself gives strength and power to His people. Blessed be God!
